channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 11th Sunday of Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 11th Sunday of Pentecost

item.title: 11th Sunday of Pentecost, 2013-09-08
item.description: :11th Sunday of Pentecost. The Parable of the unmerciful debtor teaches us practical knowledge to help us forgive others. We present many practical ways of thinking and acting that help us forgive others, based upon the mystical meaning of the parable.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :11th Sunday of Pentecost. The Parable of the unmerciful debtor teaches us practical knowledge to help us forgive others. We present many practical ways of thinking and acting that help us forgive others, based upon the mystical meaning of the parable.

item.title: 11th Sunday of Pentecost, Parable Of The Unmerciful Debtor, Golden Rule Restated Do Unto Others As You Would Have God Do Unto You, 2014-08-24
item.description: :11th Sunday of Pentecost. The Parable of the Unmerciful Debtor has the obvious teaching that we must forgive others or we will not be forgiven. Its meaning is much deeper than that: it reveals the nature of God, and our nature, and gives us a sure and certain guide for ALL of our behavior. In essence, this parable is the "Golden Rule" restated in its perfect form: "Do unto others as you would have God do unto you."
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :11th Sunday of Pentecost. The Parable of the Unmerciful Debtor has the obvious teaching that we must forgive others or we will not be forgiven. Its meaning is much deeper than that: it reveals the nature of God, and our nature, and gives us a sure and certain guide for ALL of our behavior. In essence, this parable is the "Golden Rule" restated in its perfect form: "Do unto others as you would have God do unto you."
